channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 297 replies — 746.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 297 • Updated: 2026-07-23 16:25 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62508158
>>62508147
Unless you're personally involved, then yes you wait. Obviously.

62508161
>>62508158
It's gonna be so great. All these billions of transactions, slowly and carefully chiseled onto the blockchain in artisan form instead of instantly happening via the ultra-high-speed existing mechanisms that have handled it for decades! And the advantages! Like, it's....you know, public! That helps! Man, what a time to be alive.

62508166
>>62508161
>blockchain is slow
>legacy systems are high-speed

This but exactly the opposite.
Also speed isn't even among the top priorities for banks. It's just a nice bonus.

62508531
>>62508131
>big financial interests just trying to get some publicity and some pops on their stock. None of this will ever see use and all crypto goes to 0
bingo
>le heckin DTCC is signalling to retail what to buy so we all become rich sars
imagine being so naive and retarded

62508587
>>62508531
If that's what you think, then why are you here?
You've been posting butthurtedly itt for 4 days now, fucking why? What's it to you?

62509118
>>62508587
why are you getting so emotional? why is it so terribly important to you to create consensus on an anonymous shitposting imageboard to the point you keep rambling on for a week straight? if LINK is such a certainty, why are you so desperate?

62509147
>>62509118
Just answer the question.

62509249
>>62509147
stop being so desperate
you are losing your izzat

62509297
CHAINLINK
$10,000 EOY
TOTAL XERPIE DEATH
XRP = XERPIES RAPED PLENTY

62509322
>>62509249
Desperate for what? I'm not the one posting for days in a thread about things I think will never happen anyway.

62510560
>>62509322
>I'm not the one posting for days in a thread about things I think will never happen anyway.
Uh, seems to me that's exactly what you've been doing. Shillposting because you really really really want to believe this bank + crypto tokenization crap is going to amount to anything (it won't)

62510566
>>62509322
izzat -50

62510724
>>62510560
You need to learn to read.

62510736
Wasn’t swift supposed to be the entire reason for links existence?

It being live and we are still in single digits after a week..

62511311
>>62510736
TgmHviIJ can't respond to you right now because his shift has ended
he'll be back in approximately 7 hours from now, that's when his daily shill posting usually begins

62511466
If this was for any other coin it would make 2x overnight

I think link is just cursed

62511532
>>62511311
>discussing your investment on an investment forum board you a..... paid shill!
why are you even in this thread? bizarre behaviour

62511535
>>62511532
*on an investment board makes you a.... paid shill
was half paying attention when i typed that out

62511541
>>62511532
Crypto is not an investment. It's a machine that moves the $ held by retards into the pockets of other people.

62511547
I bought chainlink with my dads money at 16 dollars.

When will it go back up so I can sell?

62511752
Imagine the kino on this board when both xrp/link cucks realize they've been had.

62511959
>>62511752
But will they ever admit they were wrong? I think most of them will take their bags to the grave, yelling about suppression the whole way.

62512292
>>62511541
>Crypto is not an investment.
Then why spend literal DAYS discussing one crypto in particular?

>It's a machine that moves the $ held by retards into the pockets of other people.
That's exactly how arguably the most successful stock trader describes the stock market.

62512345
>>62511752
>>62511959
CLL:
>promised to make node payment in Link mandatory
>said they would become the leading oracle
Which all came true

XRP:
>said a hundred banks including some of the biggest used them in 2018
>promised dozens of banks in 2019
>said they were taking over Swift in 2018
>claimed XRP is "at the heart of everything"
All of which were obvious lies.

The two really aren't comparable at all.

62512619
>>62512292
kek, exactly like I said
it must be hell to clock 12 hour shifts every day

62512634
>>62512619
>clocking shifts
I've been averaging 7 posts per day nigger. I'm only here because I'm interested and invested in this.

You on the other hand are here days on end for hours at a time getting angry about other people's investments.
You're either getting paid to be here, or you're legitimately insane.

62512662
>>62512345
This

62513163
>>62512634
>88 pbtid
>12 hour shifts per day everyday
>y-you're being paid, not me!
>s-stop noticing things!!
lmao

62513779
>>62513163
>1pbtid
>intimate knowledge of other posters/threads
>i totally don't just swap id's constantly to stalk people and talk shit about a project i don't own!
I think he's a little odd but you "people" are literal vermin.

62513871
>>62513779
>lurkers aren't allowed to post here reeeee
ookay 2pbtid, whatever you say!

item.title: ITT: biz scams you fell for — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: biz scams you fell for

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-23 16:44 UTC

OP

ITT: biz scams you fell for

Early replies (first 3)

62511608
I bought some of this but only a tiny amount. I never put a lot into crypto cause my thinking was if all the claims were true then I tiny bit now would be like the boomers who bought stocks in the 50s and held to today. Turns out it was a scam. smg had the biggest rubic campaign of any crypto. I'd like to think rbc is personally responsible for killing this board. I bet most posters lost their life savings on it and never came back to this dumpsterfire

62511611
>>62511608
>smg
*biz
I trade stocks lol I only visit smg anymore

62511769
Statera

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62511807
>>62511608
Nah it wasn't really /biz/ people buying that, it was the newfags back then and yes they are gone. I think mostly from /pol/.

62511925
>>62511807
Half the entire board was rubic posting

62512026
I bought bayhorse and actually sold for 2x

62512100
>>62511527
Rubic Rangers Assemble

62512106
>>62511925
>he still thinks we shitcoinpost because we're bullish

62512133
MY RUBIC STAYS CUBIC

62512137
>>62512106
the problem with this idea is it only works for a few people

62512438
>>62511527
how could you not make a profit off rubic??

62512495
>>62512137
>he still thinks there was more than a few people

62512525
>>62511527
This piece of shit. With genuine meme coins, I know what I'm getting into. But a compliant utility coins that at the time looks like a safe bet, but turns out doesn't fulfill it's promised utility and have their team (or lack of it) fumbles everything, feels more scammy.

There should be an active /biz/ thread dedicated to everest right now where someone shamelessly samefagging the entire thread which sounds more like a cry for help.

62512594
>>62511527
kek havnt seen this thing in a while

62513188
>>62511925
They had paid posters.

62513213
>>62511527
chainlink

62513215
i was young and retarded

62513320
token.com

62513345
>>62511527
B-But it’s still cubic right???

62513361
RLC, BNT, TIA, INJ, SXT
Probably the worst buys I’ve ever made

62513421
>>62511527

62513553
the janitor crypto

62513562
>>62511527
say what you want about it but the memes were great

62513824
>>62513562
pic
>>62513553
jew scam

62513857
>rugbic
>fartshit
>gurt
>goose finance (my favorite)

62513910
>>62511527

62513915
Jibrel Network Token

62513925
The google of blockchain

62513964
>>62513925
Thankfully I bought this at $1 and sold at $2. If only I would have done this same thing with my other shitcoins…

item.title: AirFox — 73 replies — 70.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
AirFox

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:48 UTC

OP

Any qrd on what’s going on with this thing? What exactly are these guys hoping to accomplish? Crypto is cooked and they keep trying to change this project in ways that don’t seem to matter. The TG link also doesn’t work anymore.

Early replies (first 3)

62481067
>>62480640
>2 hours later
>0 replies
jfc it’s worse than I thought. These threads used to reach bump limits after a couple hours.

62481088
>>62480640
it's a dead shitcoin, the devs are just waiting for the few final bagholders to give up so they can wrap it up and enjoy the money they made selling this shit

62481280
>>62480640
its just a rebrand and a relaunch with shifty eyes about robinhood instead of base.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62490948
>>62489045
>Which one of you fucks is selling?
lmao, you must be new here

62491616
I just noticed that 60m wallet that’s selling is an early wallet that has ties to paw / shib related stuff. I’m getting ‘frens and supporters’ vibes with this one. It’s likely the team knows who owns that wallet. It’s never going to end…..

62493068
and with that…avi’s story has come to a close. happy third birthday aviator. the final day . i am done. goodbye. 3 years too long. it’s not cozy anymore its just sad. i just cant do this anymore im sorry

62493393
>>62493068
>im out
The last sells were $48 and $4. That you?

62493444
>>62493393
no its not even worth it for me to go through the process of selling, im just done with the tg and having any hope at all for the team. its obvious they cannot find success in anything they make. sure they made stuff that works but nobody cares outside of 3 active users in tg. nothing can save this coin or the products, nobody cares. nobody uses the arcade, nobody uses the bridge. they fumbled every step of the way and it’s too far gone. stixil is vibe coding the stratus and it looks like everyone else left? we need a wellness check on pengo

62493696
>>62493444
I don’t know what is left of the team. It would be nice to at least know that much.
>no one cares
I agree but what narratives are left that people care about? It’s all been done before. It’s not even an issue with avi imo it’s the whole market. Most of the volume out there is fake. Things that seem to be doing good are really not. Wash trading and other manipulative tactics are used to make it look like crypto is alive. I believe stixil genuinely tried to make a legit project. Crypto is a tough space to build in and be successful. The ex-shib narrative was really the only thing they had going for it hem in regards to connections but ultimately it didn’t do anything.

62494825
>>62480640
I told you THIS SHIT WAS GOING TO GO TO ZERO.
WHY DID YOU NOT SELL? WHY?

62494980
WTFWT

62495070
>>62494825
I bought at $300k and my $100 has nearly doubled since. Cope and seethe tranny

62495627
make the price go up now. stixil you owe me that much atleast. let me exit with my pride.

62495664
>>62495627
The price is 2x from where it was yesterday. This is your last chance. Don’t get caught round tripping again

62497002
>>62495664
Check the price. The early friends and supporters strike again KEKW!

62497279
Idk what this coin does but I like cute anthro foxes. This is a fox business board.

62497771
>>62480640
I honestly don’t even know what’s happening with it. I bought a little over $100 worth over the past year and I can hardly get an accurate read on its present value. Am I just a boomer or did I get scammed (or both)?

62497947
>>62497771
they pulled the liquidity but they didn't run so that's a good sign on it not being a scam, they did it by doing an airdrop. They say they are still solvent but that just means that in conjunction with pulling the liquidity shows that avf is on probably running on fumes and doesn't have much operational budget left, Unless it can see some success. That said I think the arcade beta is actually really good.

Stixil's problem is that he care's too much about the chart and not enough about the business and makes some decisions to help the chart, that end up hurting the business.

I'm cautiously optimistic. Optimistic since I do think the arcade is a good idea. but i'm also cautious because of having to reassure solvency just means its something that stixil thinks that people would be worried about, which means its something that he might be worried about, which means we're running out of runway.

62497950
Stixil is playing games with my money now. either make the price go up or give me my refund. nothing you promised me happened, where is my cent per avi? you promised me my cent and now it’s time to pay up. who is going to buy my coins now? how about you give all of your holders USDC at value of their AVI holdings and be done with this little circus? it’s time to pay up your time has come to pay us

62498053
>>62497947
It really does feel like it’s running on fumes at this point. Realistically, what else is there left for stixil to do? V1, V2, the arcade, the audits, all these things were supposed to be yuge. Now we have to wait for stratus to launch in Q3 but what’s the point. They will make an x post announcing the launch of stratus and only 10 people will care. Then what? Just sit around hoping that people get interested if the market recovers? Everything avi is trying to do has already been done for the most part.

62498181
Personal opinion, for me celebrating 3 years by rugging holders isnt the best look. A 60m+ holder dumped his bag, which i assume is due to this action or some falling out. Signaling they are out of funds (red flag) then requesting users that already got rug pulled via liquidity grab to then put in more money for liquidity (another red flag). Perhaps all i see is red flags and there are some green flags but its not looking good

62501458
>>62498053
The purpose of producing some kind of product like this is to get some exchange to adopt it, no way in hell normies are going to care about skybridge

I've already accepted that i burned my money

62501920
>>62501458
Exchanges are not interested. Avi has been around for 3 years now. The hint that coinbase was showing interest was a lie. Not necessarily from the team but from CB themselves. Apparently it’s something they did to a lot of projects.

Let’s be honest here. Stixil isn’t coming up with, let alone building, a solution to all of cryptos problems. Stratus will be the final chapter to this project. I can’t see anyone sticking around after that, holders and the team. It just is t worth the time or money

62501921
>>62481067
>>62480640
Dead shitcoin

62505034
he could make the price go up anytime he wanted. yet he doesn’t. i gave you 3 years of my life and money. stixil do what is right, now.

62507620
Great now coffee is dumping with his 150m bag

62507779
>>62483375
I bought some of this and then tried to play the arcade games which sucked so I sold but somehow made a few hundred dollars. And then I’d check into the threads and see a bunch of cope and I would lol.

62508294
>>62507620
Now tainttickler is selling as well. There’s no way this token sees above 1m mcap ever again.

62509076
im out. 3 years later and I have sold everything. goodbye aviator goodbye stixil goodbye nill. 3 years too long. congratulations aviators, you made it!

62509231
>>62509076
Before you go what was your favorite game in the Airfox Arcade?

62513582
why do they think people will care about stratus? No one cared about the bridge no one cared about the arcade

62513669
>>62513582
>why do they think people will care about stratus?
At this point, I don’t believe they think that. Stixil is probably the only core member left too. I wouldn’t be surprised to see people one clipping their entire bag after stratus launches. There really is no incentive to hold this with only $500 in daily volume, if that. I think we should just let this thread die already. There isn’t much else to talk about

62513680
>>62513669
>There isn’t much else to talk about
But no one here has even mentioned what theyve been playing in the arcade yet? This thread still has plenty of life in it. What games are you playing?

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 91 replies — 70.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:32 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496951
>>62495641
slowly

62497289
>>62486528
great job man

62497294
Its very interesting to see the current transition of this board from being a crypto board to becoming a stock market board.
More and more anons slowly realizing crypto potential gains just arent there anymore and we might even be in a secular bear market.
If stock start crashing, crypto will have the biggest bear that it has ever seen.

62498541
>>62497294
It's the best time to accumulate then

62498999
>>62475235
bullish

62500137
>>62491816
How do you do that?

62500294
>>62500137
You can check the 200 week moving average on trading view, we are currently holding it as support. Any purchases below that average have given great returns.

62500310
>>62475235
Checked out sitting on mostly cash with about 20% in it right now.
Setting a significant buy order at 55k, if it drops a lot more I'll FOMO whatever I can otw down.
If that doesn't happen, I'll start DCAing again in mid/late September and begin incrementing the DCA in Nov.
If none of this makes sense, who cares, this is what my retard brain is telling me is a good plan.

62501164
>>62500294
Still not a guarantee

62501169
>>62475258
Never forget that Ben took his sexy assistant to Michael Saylor's 100k party, and not his wife.

62501528
>>62484050
I like your approach...

62501530
>>62501169
If you have that money, you can do that

62501533
>>62501169
this is why dating the ugly dork as a woman doesnt even work

he will cheat on you as soon as he can like any other man

62501560
>>62501533
Money speaks

62501981
>>62500310
You're smart

62502237
kek

62502257
>>62501164
There's no such thing as a guarantee when it comes to investment.

62503437
>>62502257
As always...

62503861
Bitcoin isn't going to survive long term. Its the most technologically challenged coin and has the most complex problem to overcome with Y2Q (post quantum). POW cant stop quantum computers from minning, POS can. Bitcoin has to defend from two fronts and big upgrades isn't what the project is know for. It took Eth 5 years to switch their consensus to POS and Eth has 3 times more active developers than Bitcoin, so good luck bitchuds Y2Q is will proceed on April 14, 2030.

62504863
>>62503861
Are you sure?

62504896
>>62476239
Always?
In 2020 he said BTC wouldn't reach 20k until 2022 or early 2023. In 2021 he said ETH would reach at least 10k before the bear market. In late 2021 he said the absolute bottom target for the bear market was $40k BTC. Don't forget all his videos shilling ADA with $10 ADA clickbait titles.

62506524
>>62504896
It's easy to say something

62506580
still DCA'ing

62506855
>>62503861
Nigga all this FUD about quantum niggers is just niggertybabbltygook we have bigger issues than money and Bitcorns if SHA-256 or the curves nigger word synthesis or what ever other worry you fags have is broke in an hour

62508173
>>62506855
Why worry?

62508391
waiting for this champ to do his thing

62509707
>>62508391
when will he buy again?

62510364
>>62506855
It's just a fud

62512109
>>62510364
I guess so

62513628
>>62506580
Nice

item.title: Cloud compute — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cloud compute

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-23 16:49 UTC

OP

Google released earnings yesterday and had a big cloud beat

Here’s my question: if every hyper scaler is now using their data centers for cloud compute (MSFT,AMZN,GOOGL,META)

At some point supply > demand and the margins of cloud go down, no? When it was just AWS and azure I could understand the margins, but if we keep building hundreds of billions of data centers, and AI turns out to not be intensive, wouldn’t the cloud compute business get eviscerated via margin
compression?

Doesn’t this literally kill MSFT and MSFT?

Early replies (first 3)

62513639
>>62513631

*kill MSFT and AMZN

62513657
>>62513631
No. MSFT’s shitty CEO kills MSFT. As for AMZN, they’ll be fine.

62513713
>>62513631
Datacentres are a bad business
High energy costs
Weather viunerable
high insurance
and the network compute, GPS, CPUs and RAM going into them depreciates to zero in 4-5 years

Software WAS a great business, nearly 100% margin over staff costs, low distribution costs, basically legal immunity on product faults etc

Being a datacentre landlord selling it as a service is a shit business. Go look up what happened to the last generation of datacentre companies like Sunguard.

Never ever invest in anything with a jeet CEO

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62513728
>>62513657
Amazons retail business was built on selling cheap crap from China and is effectively over.

Beyond that they are a datacentre landlord as well, as is meta (spyware and ads) and google (with an ad agency and spyware business bolted on).

Shit business models. Advertising and marketing is a vastly overvalued industry as well.

62513923
>>62513631
They could just all mine Bitcoin

62513960
>>62513631
All I know is that a new generation of chips—much less power-hungry—is coming to market soon. Chinese chips, neuromorphic chips, etc.

62513981
>>62513960
Then your full of shit and a bad business is a bad business. ASML makes the stuff tha makes the chips. China is locked out and going nowhere and staying firmly 10-15 years behind and is late to a bubble that is bursting not becuase of tech but because of shiller cape and retarded jeet ceo business models and duplicitous earnings and financing.

item.title: AI sabotages my attempts to make money — 21 replies — 20.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI sabotages my attempts to make money

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:47 UTC

OP

Every time I try to develop a bot/trading strategy to make money on crypto platforms like Polymarket, Hyperliquid, GMGN (Solana) etc - I get negatively biased and pessimistic responses from Opus 4.8 - that there is no way to make money online on these platforms, and the ones who make money are just "survivorship bias". I tried Fable, but my requests are constantly blocked, even the simplest tasks like research or code reviews. I have a feeling that this game is somehow rigged and the AI wont help me to make significant money - like there is some internal censorship/block programmed in these AI models.

Early replies (first 3)

62509570
>>62509553
You're not gonna get results from trying to use public cloud models like this

62509583
>>62509553
They won't let u make serious money + this would threaten the whole system.

62509595
>>62509553
Claude knows what he's doing. Trust in him. Maybe try being nicer next time as well.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62509618
>>62509583
Very nice pic

62509999
>>62509570
What models would u recommend?

62510008
>>62509553
Quads and I will chop my cock off

62510113
>>62509553
Just try harder or upgrade your subscription.

62510135
>>62509553
I coded an AI agent using LangGraph and Grok 4.1 Fast on Hyperliquid. There were no insurmountable issues; it was just a bit dumb at first, and getting it to set stop-loss and take-profit orders was tricky. I’m still working on it. I used Gemini to help with the coding—it generated a large chunk of the code for me. Hope this helps.

62510378
>>62509618
Generated by AI

62510413
>businesses want people to use AI for business, then pay them for the subscription
>put retarded restrictions that drive people away from corporate and american AI models
Why is modern society run by mouth breathing retards?

62510552
>>62510008
Really close LMFAO

62511544
>>62509999
deepseek or kimi, but you're be doing china a favor too.

62511660
>>62509553
>government banned these AIs, particularly the department of commerce for "national security reasons"
HEY WHY WONT THIS AI MAKE ME RICH?!?!?
>thats you, thats how dumb you sound
Like clockwork /biz/, never change. The system is designed so you keep slaving away so they can spend billions of taxpayer money bombing for greater (greatest ally)x3. You will have to be more crafty and pull one over on AI. It is possible, but you have to think outside all of human experience
>t. .5% of power prompter stat

62512021
>>62509553
local chads, I cannot stop enjoying my winning

62512232
>>62512021
What are you using?

62512236
>>62512021
What's your setup?

62512247
>>62509553
just run your own local 600 billion parameter ai that also has ai agent integration and internet uplink capability

62512277
>>62509553
dear ai, please autowin at the hardest game that exists in the world, against billions of other players, while i understand next to nothing (im hoping you will)

62512754
>>62512277
(And those other players also use AI)

62512858
>>62512277
I didn't say auto win - I deed some deep research, parsed relevant Discord groups etc and based on that tried dozens of strategies across dozens of sessions. Every time the result is the same: no profitable strategy is found. Opus becomes so biased that he tells that Polymarket and Hyperliquid are casino-like scam altogether, and there is no way to even make 50$ per day.
So something is definitely off here; either there is some filtering or the "AI" part is extremely overrated and there's no intelligence at all.

62513679
>>62512858
> how could the AI tell me that gambling is casino-like scam

item.title: Pitch your business idea — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Pitch your business idea

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-23 16:36 UTC

OP

Wagecucking is unsustainable.
AI will take your job.
The government will take what's left.
Your only option is to start your own business before it's too late.
Post your business ideas and rate/roast others.
I'll go first
>Men's private social club
>Men need friends
>Men need a third space
>Men need camaraderie, brotherhood, and the stuff JP talks about, just without the faggotry
>Men need networking, and professional connections to get ahead
>Subscription model, bar, non-profit

Early replies (first 3)

62513491
Making indie games

62513836
>>62513456
>Men's private social club
I already have one of those, the local airfield. Only high performing gentlemen of reasonable means who are not afraid of death.

62513863
Topless maid service.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62513906
>>62513863
and then I also open a competing, Bottomless maid service.

62513921
>>62513456
My real plan is to open an upscale bar in a Southeast Asian country and lead a comfortable, modest life while continuing to invest. Nothing else—just a realistic and ordinary plan.

62509287

The ROI is not there anymore but it will bounce back around november and do a x3.
Thenif you live in most European countries, you'll take a 30% taxes on that gain.

62509474
>>62502209

All they care about is bread and circus.
Unemployed and broke techies knew about it, but basically hated it because they couldn't invest. Like an incel, who hates women because he can't have one.

62509481
>>62509198

Not if you chose the right coin, and are willing to wait 25 years.

62509509

Basically like the Dotcom bubble which was repeated with the recent AI bubble.

62509598
>>62508683

62509851
>>62509167

62509855

62511221

The crash this year is going to be biblical. Thankfully I convinced my family to liquidate almost 80% of their holdings to get ready for the deal of the century coming up.

item.title: Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no… — 159 replies — 92.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no…

Open thread • Replies: 159 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:06 UTC

OP

Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in now.

Early replies (first 3)

62483359
>>62483350
4%

62483366
>>62483359
?

62483376
>>62483366
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62510630
>>62510541
>orbital data-centers

62510633
>>62510620
>Look honey, I insulted someone without getting bad karma, can you tell it to your boyfriend ?

62510646
>>62483350
>$260
no
>$150
likely. if you bought the ipo thats on you bro

62510653
>>62510633

62510659
>>62510653

62510692
>>62510541
do the math for me.

like actually:
How much does each launch cost?
take the total cost of an orbital data center and divide it by the total PB of the center (would use EB, but the math would be too unweildy)
be sure to include the costs of the solar power panels and anything else.
Remember that you will need abotu 2sq meters of panel per PB, so 10,000 sq meters of solar panels. the cost of the panels is relatively reasonable. the cost of getting them into space undamaged and then assembling them does not seem as reasonable
Now figure out how much income that data center makes per PB (on earth, the rental rate per PB is about $2500/month, but that includes $1000+ in power usage) and then you discount for profit for the renter.

so lets say it is a 5 EB orbital center, so a little over 6000 PB makes maybe $4.2 million/month

each launch costs abotu $75 million, so it would take 18 months of profiit to pay for one launch. I estimate it would take 18 months to pay for each launch.

Assume they get 50% more efficient at scale, that is still 9 months of operations per launch to equal one terrestrial data center.

Now factor in india launching at 10% the cost. How will space x make any money?


Now do the cost of repairs and upgrades. A benefit of a terrestrial datacenter is that you just send bob and achmed over in a truck to upgrade. Now do that in space.

62510713
>>62510620
cap ex is a type of ex.

ex burns cash

you don't burn ex

so you don't be "burning up capex"

you are burning up cash on capex

Bro - this is a /biz/ thread People should understand how to use terms like capex more precisely than "they insist in burning up CapEx on a dumb AI" when they should be saying :

>No one touches spacex because they burn up too much cash on capex for stupid AI.

62511526
>>62509948
Closed at $115. Fair value probably around at most $70

62512073
>>62510606

>the military doesn’t have access to the internet or probably an entire different network for only military interfaces

The cope is strong baggie

62512276
>>62512073
The military has their own satellites but they are much fewer in number and have less capability as a result. SpaceX is the first to build a megaconstellation, which was only feasible because of their advancements in reusable boosters.

The military actually wanted to build a megaconstellation for Brilliant Pebbles in the 1980s, but it just wasn't feasible with technology of the time.

62512552
>>62511526
more like 30 lmao

62512843
>>62512073
>the military doesn’t have access to the internet or probably an entire different network for only military interfaces
I know you're just being a pedantic faggot, but one of the biggest contracts starlink has is the US military. If you knew anything you'd be aware of the starlink controversy that happened not long ago regarding supplying/cutting off internet to conflict areas of the Ukraine.
Starlink is the one good product SpaceX has done that actually makes money.

62512847
Can we break new lows today boys?

62512852
>>62512552
If you want to see what SPCX chart will look kike for the next years/decades, look at FCEL (FuelCell. a chronically unprofitable green energy pump & dump. same profitability & pipedream profile. It's a perpetual destruction of value

62512871
You know it's a great stock when there's a baggie cope general on /biz/. This might as well be the start of a recurring thread. AI data centers...IN SPACE

62512950
floor is 112.67

62513053

62513145
>>62513053
spacex + starlink is around 30, spacex + starlink + XAI (+ tesla soon) is around 5, anything above that is overvauled

62513181
>>62512871
In space!!!!!!!!! KEK

62513201
>>62512871
Day 1 of posting SPCX until it goes back up to $120

62513206
Spacebros....

62513304
no one here actually bought this right? you're all just memeing

62513330
>>62513304
I bought 6X bear and got assraped when it pumped 2 days ago. Almost made it back shorting more though.

I knew this was ass-backwards from the beginning. An AI firm (or whatever the fuck spcx is) with nothing to show worth 1 trillion has to have a hell of an engineering team. So then that team buys cursor for 80 billion because they can't figure out how to shove an LLM into vscode which exposes them as frauds. That's really all it took for me to bet against this shitpile hard.

62513383

-4% daily was chikkenfeed, time to watch this sucker DIE

62513470

and Musk is going bust.
Please, please merge these shitfests (SPCX+TSLA) - not a single fucking honest prophet to be found between them.

62513530
>>62513470

if you aren't buying at these discounted prices you're a fool

62510483
>>62507495
>>62508076
We have a dog and it is expensive and out vet likes to guilt shame us when we are like "no, we aren't going to pay $8500 for you to treat her slow growing tumor that has been there for 3 years and is still only the size of an apricot.

Also we have 2-3 cats at any time and go to different shelters to adopt since the one asked me why I wanted a 5th cat in two years and then freaked out when I said that coyotes and foxes keep eating them.

Cats are for rodent control. Dogs are for deer control (and coyote/fox/possum/raccoon control)

62510516
>>62507660

And the race that was created by non-white women, brown arranged marriages and patriarchal societies. Biological evolution is going to start developing fast again now that white women are allowed to do their natural instinct eugenetics, even tho population sizes will get smaller.

62510519

62510525

62511081

I have met a grand total of 1 coal burner who fetishized niggers, and 1 other rice burner who fetishized gooks+chinks. I'm a well connected guy socially, i've met a staggering number of people in my life, the vast majority of race mixing women are just desperate, lonely, and not picky about appearance, which is why they let a clingy shitskin get close in the first place. 62513494

>There is no day of reckoning coming,
That day of reckoning is menopause for unmarried, childless, undesirable women who will be the majority in 10 years. They will, quite literally, die alone, and spend their dwindling years screeching about men and drowning on NSAIDs.

But we also haven't had a really big war in 100 years, so maybe this is the necessary genetic cleansing we've been missing. All the I'm-too-good-for-you females will just be genetically deleted, leaving only marriageable women behind in the future.
